tossups.question: In a coloratura aria accompanied by piano, a soprano character with this profession sings about her many flings to cheer up a “mighty princess.” Another soprano in this profession repeats the words “e van!” (“eh VAHN”) to end an F-sharp major aria in which she trills to imitate birds. An opera company clashes with Zerbinetta and other characters with this profession in Ariadne auf Naxos. A woman with this profession is frightened by the “fire” in her husband’s gaze but comforted by screeching birds in the aria (*) “Stridono lassù.” (“stree-doh-NOH la-SOO”) Tonio reminds the audience that people in this profession “are men… like you” in that opera’s prologue, which begins with the words, “Si può?” Another character with this profession stabs his wife Nedda and her lover Silvio before declaring, “La commedia è finita!” Canio sings “Vesti la giubba” (“JOO-bah”) before going to work as, for 10 points, what title profession of Ruggero Leoncavallo’s opera Pagliacci (“pa-LYAH-chee”)?
tossups.answer: commedia dell’arte actors [or commedia dell’arte performers; accept clowns or comedians; accept pagliacci or pagliaccio until “Pagliacci” is read; reject “(court) jesters” or “fools”]
tossups.answer_sanitized: commedia dell'arte actors [or commedia dell'arte performers; accept clowns or comedians; accept pagliacci or pagliaccio until "Pagliacci" is read; reject "(court) jesters" or "fools"]
tossups.question_sanitized: In a coloratura aria accompanied by piano, a soprano character with this profession sings about her many flings to cheer up a "mighty princess." Another soprano in this profession repeats the words "e van!" ("eh VAHN") to end an F-sharp major aria in which she trills to imitate birds. An opera company clashes with Zerbinetta and other characters with this profession in Ariadne auf Naxos. A woman with this profession is frightened by the "fire" in her husband's gaze but comforted by screeching birds in the aria (*) "Stridono lassu." ("stree-doh-NOH la-SOO") Tonio reminds the audience that people in this profession "are men... like you" in that opera's prologue, which begins with the words, "Si puo?" Another character with this profession stabs his wife Nedda and her lover Silvio before declaring, "La commedia e finita!" Canio sings "Vesti la giubba" ("JOO-bah") before going to work as, for 10 points, what title profession of Ruggero Leoncavallo's opera Pagliacci ("pa-LYAH-chee")?

tossups.question: In a scene from this opera, a choir sings the words "don, din, don" to mimic the sound of vesper bells. A character in this opera furiously repeats "il nome! il nome!" after discovering two lovers planning to run away at midnight. The audience is asked to "mark well our souls...for we are men...like you" by a single character on stage in a "prologue" to this opera. That character from this opera overhears a woman imitating birdsong as she sings the aria "Stridono lassù." Tonio exposes an affair in this opera to a character who shouts "La commedia è finita!" after stabbing his wife Nedda and her lover Silvio. Canio sings the aria "Vesti la giubba" in, for 10 points, what verismo opera by Ruggero Leoncavallo about a troupe of clowns?
tossups.answer_sanitized: I Pagliacci [accept The Clowns until "clowns" is read]
tossups.question_sanitized: In a scene from this opera, a choir sings the words "don, din, don" to mimic the sound of vesper bells. A character in this opera furiously repeats "il nome! il nome!" after discovering two lovers planning to run away at midnight. The audience is asked to "mark well our souls...for we are men...like you" by a single character on stage in a "prologue" to this opera. That character from this opera overhears a woman imitating birdsong as she sings the aria "Stridono lassu." Tonio exposes an affair in this opera to a character who shouts "La commedia e finita!" after stabbing his wife Nedda and her lover Silvio. Canio sings the aria "Vesti la giubba" in, for 10 points, what verismo opera by Ruggero Leoncavallo about a troupe of clowns?

tossups.question: At the beginning of an Act II aria from this opera, a nearly offstage singer tells a woman to “open your window to me [who is] close at hand, calling and sighing for you.” Villagers in this opera sing an Act I “bell chorus” that precedes a “ballatella” inspired by the sight of screeching birds, “Stridono lassu.” A man dressed as the servant Taddeo asks the question “Si può?” at the beginning of this opera, in which (*) Arlecchino sings “O Colombina.” In this opera, Nedda is stabbed by Canio, who sings the aria “Vesti la giubba” while putting on his costume. For 10 points, name this Ruggiero Leoncavallo opera typically paired with Cavalleria Rusticana and titled for a group of clowns.
tossups.question_sanitized: At the beginning of an Act II aria from this opera, a nearly offstage singer tells a woman to "open your window to me [who is] close at hand, calling and sighing for you." Villagers in this opera sing an Act I "bell chorus" that precedes a "ballatella" inspired by the sight of screeching birds, "Stridono lassu." A man dressed as the servant Taddeo asks the question "Si puo?" at the beginning of this opera, in which (*) Arlecchino sings "O Colombina." In this opera, Nedda is stabbed by Canio, who sings the aria "Vesti la giubba" while putting on his costume. For 10 points, name this Ruggiero Leoncavallo opera typically paired with Cavalleria Rusticana and titled for a group of clowns.

tossups.question: Stephen Barlow changed the setting of this opera to the 1970s in his current production of it. Foreboding ensues after one character offers to stay behind to groom a donkey. Another character in this opera, which takes place on the Feast of the Assumption, picks up a bullwhip to ward off the advances of a lovestruck man and joins in with a singing bird in the aria "Stridono lassu." That character is stabbed to death in this opera's second act, after which her killer declares that "the comedy is over." Canio laments having to act jovial despite being tormented by Nedda's infidelity in this opera's aria "Vesti la Giubba." For 10 points, name this opera that dramatizes the love affairs within a group of circus performers, by Ruggero Leoncavallo.
tossups.question_sanitized: Stephen Barlow changed the setting of this opera to the 1970s in his current production of it. Foreboding ensues after one character offers to stay behind to groom a donkey. Another character in this opera, which takes place on the Feast of the Assumption, picks up a bullwhip to ward off the advances of a lovestruck man and joins in with a singing bird in the aria "Stridono lassu." That character is stabbed to death in this opera's second act, after which her killer declares that "the comedy is over." Canio laments having to act jovial despite being tormented by Nedda's infidelity in this opera's aria "Vesti la Giubba." For 10 points, name this opera that dramatizes the love affairs within a group of circus performers, by Ruggero Leoncavallo.

tossups.question: One character in this work sings a ballatella about the freedom of the birds in the scene following the "Bell Chorus." In another scene, a character shouts "Name of God! Those same words" after hearing a woman say "I will always be yours!" for the second time. After discovering his wife's infidelity, one character tells himself to "laugh at the grief that poisons your heart" and perform the aria's title action "because people have paid money and want to be amused." That aria, "Vesti la giubba," is sung by Canio, who stabs Nedda while she is playing the role of Columbine. For 10 points, name this Leoncavallo opera frequently performed alongside Cavalleria Rusticana.
tossups.answer_sanitized: Pagliacci [or I Pagliacci; or Players; or Clowns]

tossups.question: A recording by Enrico Caruso of one aria from this opera was the first record in history to sell a million copies. The Intermezzo of this opera echoes the baritone solo that opened it. The lead soprano in this opera sings an aria in admiration of birds in the sky, “Stridono lassu”. One character is angered by another saying “I am yours always” as Colombina to a character playing Arlecchino in a play within this opera. At the beginning of this opera, the protagonist warns Tonio not to pursue his wife Nedda. This opera’s protagonist, Canio, sings the aria “Vesti la Giubba” as he puts on his costume at the end of Act I. For 10 points, name this Leoncavallo opera typically paired with Cavalleria Rusticana that is about a clown.
tossups.answer: I Pagliacci [accept “Clowns” before the last word of the tossup]
tossups.answer_sanitized: I Pagliacci [accept "Clowns" before the last word of the tossup]

Tonio, in character as Taddeo, sings these two Italian words twice to the audience’s “ladies” and “gentlemen” to kick off the prologue of Pagliacci (“pall-YAH-chee”). These two words precede “morir” four times in the final stanza of “Una furtiva lagrima.”
Enrico Caruso and Luciano Pavarotti are among the most celebrated singers with this voice type to have played Canio.

answers: “si può” (“see pwoh”) [or “Si può?”; or “Si può morir”]

bonuses.leadin_sanitized: This aria includes a tradition of letting the voice crack in a sob-note at its climax. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this aria in which Canio bemoans having to perform immediately after hearing of his wife’s infidelity. This aria’s title expresses the sentiment that “the show must go on.”

Canio is a member of this profession, which titles the Leoncavallo opera in which he appears. In “Vesti la giubba,” he puts on the makeup of this profession and tells himself to “turn your distress and tears into jokes.”

bonuses.leadin: Enrico Caruso’s recording of this aria was the first record to sell over a million copies. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this aria in which Canio laments that he must pretend to be happy despite his wife Nedda’s infidelity. Canio sings this aria as he dons his costume.

“Vesti la giubba” closes out the first of two acts of this verismo opera about a troupe of clowns by Ruggiero Leoncavallo (roo-ZHAIR-oh leon-ca-VA-low). Opera companies often perform this opera alongside Pietro Mascagni’s (moss-CAH-nyee’s) Cavelleria Rusticana (ca-va-LAIR-ia roos-ti-CAH-na).
In this other verismo opera by Giacomo Puccini, Baron Scarpia executes Cavaradossi despite his promise to the title character, who throws herself off a tower to her death.

bonuses.leadin: Enrico Caruso’s 1902 recording of this aria was the first record to sell over 1 million copies. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this aria sung by a character who has just learned that his wife, Nedda, is cheating on him. Although the singer of this aria encourages himself to put on his make-up despite his sadness, he ends this aria in tears.
Canio sings “Vesti la giubba” in this Ruggero Leoncavallo-composed verismo opera about a commedia dell’arte troupe.

This soprano performed as Nedda in the well-received 1893 Covent Garden staging of Pagliacci. She was Covent Garden’s chief soprano for most of the late 19th century and famously refused to sing in German-language operas.
answers_sanitized: Dame Nellie Melba [or Helen Porter Mitchell]
